description_long: This object is a vintage television with a boxy, rectangular wooden cabinet and a large, dark glass screen dominating the front face. The front panel features two rotary knobs on either side and a row of four circular push buttons beneath the screen, all with a worn metallic finish. The sides of the television are smooth, with one side displaying a vented metal grille for ventilation. The back panel is equipped with a protruding cathode ray tube (CRT) neck, ventilation holes, and printed technical information, giving it a distinctly retro appearance. The overall finish is matte brown wood, with metallic and plastic accents on the controls and vents. This television would likely be found in an urban setting as a discarded appliance, a retro collectible, or a prop in a period scene.
description: A vintage, boxy wooden television with a large dark screen, metallic knobs, and a vented back panel. The design features a matte brown finish and retro control buttons.
description_view_0: Side view showing the smooth wooden panel and a vented metal grille.
description_view_1: Rear view displaying the protruding CRT neck, ventilation holes, and technical markings.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with a plain, unadorned wooden surface and the CRT neck barely visible.
description_view_3: Front view featuring the large dark screen, two rotary knobs, and four metallic push buttons.
attribute_bezel_design: thick, brown wooden bezel with a matte finish and rounded corners
attribute_control_button_layout: two rotary knobs and four push buttons arranged in a row below the screen on the front panel
